definition: Nutriment or sustaining intake, especially in the doctrinal analysis of what keeps experience and becoming going.
notes: The project prefers nutriment because it works across physical and non-physical cases in the canonical fourfold set. Food is acceptable where the context is plainly literal or when English needs a lighter word. The term should be heard as doctrinally broad sustenance, not only edible intake.

context_rules.context: In doctrinal analysis of the four nutriments
In plainly literal contexts or lighter explanatory prose
When the passage emphasizes the fourfold analysis of what sustains experience and becoming
context_rules.notes: Keep the doctrinal breadth of the category explicit.

example_phrases.translation: bhikkhus, these are the four nutriments
example_phrases.notes: Standard opening to the nutriment analysis.
Useful for the causal framing around nutriment in SN 12.
